title: Mildly Yeasty Algerian Dough Flatbread
description: A traditional Algerian flatbread featuring a yeasty and chewy texture, perfect for accompanying meals or as a snack, made with cold water and cooked in a tandoor for an authentic touch, and ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients for a comforting experience.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, yeast, salt, and sugar.
Gradually add cold water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the tandoor or a regular oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Brush the dough circles with olive oil and cook in the preheated tandoor or oven for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Remove the flatbreads from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
